channel.title: dreamclerk blog
channel.description: field notes from building dreamclerk. the data, the rejects, the rubric changes. nothing polished after the fact.

item.title: the 90-second internship interview that changed 14% of outcomes
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: we ran a 90-second phone screen on 1,200 internship applicants. 14% got a callback. here is what we changed, and what we got wrong.
item.content:encoded: we ran a 90-second phone screen on 1,200 internship applicants. 14% got a callback. here is what we changed, and what we got wrong.

item.title: inside our bias audit — the rubric, the data, the changes
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: every quarter we run a bias audit on the dreamclerk interview. here is the rubric, the per-group pass-rates, and the three rubric changes we made after cohort 1.
item.content:encoded: every quarter we run a bias audit on the dreamclerk interview. here is the rubric, the per-group pass-rates, and the three rubric changes we made after cohort 1.

item.title: how to pass a coding interview with no experience
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: no internships. no github. no leetcode streak. here is the 4-step protocol we built to prep dreamclerk applicants in 6 weeks — and what the data says about it.
item.content:encoded: no internships. no github. no leetcode streak. here is the 4-step protocol we built to prep dreamclerk applicants in 6 weeks — and what the data says about it.

item.title: the in-browser ide — what it actually runs, what it can't
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: monaco on top of a webcontainer. 200ms cold start. 12mb of wasm. here is the architecture, the security model, and the 3 things it cannot do.
item.content:encoded: monaco on top of a webcontainer. 200ms cold start. 12mb of wasm. here is the architecture, the security model, and the 3 things it cannot do.

item.title: shipping code vs knowing code — a 5-minute glossary
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: the 14 terms every dreamclerk applicant should be able to use in a sentence, with a worked example for each. bookmark this. re-read it before the interview.
item.content:encoded: the 14 terms every dreamclerk applicant should be able to use in a sentence, with a worked example for each. bookmark this. re-read it before the interview.
